dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: System overview
dashboard.yourQuotaSubtitle: Resource usage vs. limits for your account
dashboard.yourQuotaFootnote: Users, nodes, and hosts are lifetime change counters — they increment on every create and on every delete, so the number only ever grows. Traffic is different: it represents the total traffic you are responsible for, equal to the sum of every limit you've allocated plus the traffic your users have already consumed. Creating a user adds their limit. Resetting a user adds the amount they had used — that consumed traffic is on your tab because the user just got a fresh quota. Editing a user down (when used is below the new limit) returns the difference. Deleting a user returns only the portion they hadn't used; the consumed portion stays on your tab forever.

violations.tooltip: The anti-abuse system analyzes user connections and detects anomalies: suspicious geo-movements, multiple ASNs, unusual devices and other deviations. Score from 0 to 100 shows the degree of suspicion.

severityTooltips.critical: Score 80–100. Serious anomalies requiring immediate intervention.
severityTooltips.low: Score 0–39. Informational notices, no action needed.

constructor.newSubtitle: Create a rule that will automatically perform actions when certain events occur
constructor.triggerHint: Define the trigger condition. The rule will fire automatically when the selected event occurs.
constructor.eventHint: Event trigger responds to specific real-time system actions. The rule fires immediately after the event occurs.
constructor.minScoreHint: Severity filter. Rule will only fire if the violation score is at least this value. Leave empty to react to any violations.
constructor.minOfflineHint: Delay before triggering. Rule fires only if the node is down longer than the specified time. Helps avoid false positives from brief outages.
constructor.scheduleHint: Rule will run periodically on schedule. Use CRON for precise scheduling or a simple interval for repeating tasks.
constructor.thresholdHint: Threshold trigger monitors a numeric metric and fires when the value reaches the set threshold. Checked periodically.
constructor.metricConditionHint: Rule will fire when metric "{{metric}}" matches the condition
constructor.categoryHint: Category helps group rules for easy search and filtering.
constructor.categoryAutoHint: Suggested automatically, but you can change it.
constructor.conditionsHint: Optional step. Add conditions for more precise filtering. Rule fires only if ALL specified conditions are met simultaneously (AND logic). Without conditions, the rule fires on every trigger event.
constructor.noConditionsHint: Click "Add condition" to fine-tune or "Next" to continue without filters
constructor.actionHint: Choose the action the system will perform automatically when the trigger and conditions are met.
constructor.notifyHint: Specify where and what message to send when the rule fires
constructor.webhookHint: Data will be sent as a POST request in JSON format
constructor.blockReasonHint: This reason will be recorded in the user's profile and visible to admins. If not specified, the default reason will be used.
constructor.targetAutomatic: Target user is determined automatically from the trigger
constructor.blockScheduleWarn: Blocking by schedule requires an event or threshold trigger to determine the user
constructor.blockWarning: Warning: blocked user will lose access to the service
constructor.disableUserHint: User account will be deactivated. User won't be able to connect, but their data will be preserved. Account can be re-enabled manually.
constructor.disableScheduleWarn: For schedules, use "Cleanup expired" — disabling a specific user requires an event or threshold trigger
constructor.cleanupHint: Users whose subscription expired more than the specified number of days ago will be disabled. This helps automatically free up resources.
constructor.restartNodeHint: A restart command will be sent. Active connections will be temporarily interrupted.
constructor.restartSelectedWarn: Brief connection interruption on the selected node
constructor.restartAllWarn: Brief connection interruption for all users
constructor.enable_nodeSelectedWarn: The selected node will be enabled
constructor.enable_nodeAllWarn: All disabled nodes will be enabled
constructor.disable_nodeSelectedWarn: The selected node will be disabled, connections dropped
constructor.disable_nodeAllWarn: All connected nodes will be disabled
constructor.resetTrafficHint: Traffic counters will be reset to zero. This will restore users' full traffic allowance per their plan.
constructor.resetTrafficScheduleWarn: Resetting traffic by schedule requires an event or threshold trigger to determine the user
constructor.forceSyncHint: Configuration on all nodes will be force-updated. Useful after bulk settings changes to ensure configuration is up to date on all servers.
constructor.ruleNameHint: Short name to identify the rule in the list
constructor.descriptionHint: Detailed description so other admins understand the rule's purpose
constructor.descriptionPlaceholder: Briefly describe the purpose of this rule
constructor.targetFromTrigger: Target: from trigger or all nodes
constructor.validationCron: Select a schedule or enter a CRON expression
constructor.validationConditions: Fill in all condition fields or remove incomplete ones

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Bot and panel configuration. Priority: DB > .env > default
settings.noSettingsHint: Make sure the database is connected and the bot has been started at least once

faq.description: Frequently asked questions and solutions to common problems

panelPort.a:
Reverse proxy issue (Nginx/Caddy). After server reboot, the proxy starts before containers are ready and can't resolve DNS names.

For Caddy — add lb_try_duration 30s to each reverse_proxy block:
reverse_proxy web-frontend:80 {
    lb_try_duration 30s
}

For Nginx — add resolver 127.0.0.11 valid=10s to the upstream block.

Quick fix: docker restart caddy (or nginx) after reboot.

passwordLogin.q: Can't log in with password after registration
passwordLogin.a:
Possible causes:
1. Password contains Cyrillic look-alike characters (С instead of C, А instead of A). Switch keyboard to EN and re-enter.
2. Browser autofilled incorrectly. Try entering manually.
3. If nothing helps — reset via CLI:
docker exec -it <container> python3 scripts/admin_cli.py reset-password --username admin

highCpu.a:
Check that indexes exist on frequently queried tables. Key indexes:
- users: idx_users_username_lower, idx_users_email_lower
- user_connections: idx_user_connections_user_active

Also configure in Performance settings:
- DB Pool max size (default 50)
- Statement timeout (default 60s)

Monitor via: SELECT * FROM pg_stat_user_tables ORDER BY seq_scan DESC;

nodeOffline.q: Node shows as offline but is actually running
nodeOffline.a:
Check:
1. Agent is running and AGENT_COLLECTOR_URL points to web-backend (port 8081)
2. Agent token matches the token in the panel
3. No firewall between node and backend
4. Server time is synchronized (NTP)

alertSpam.q: Alerts keep spamming about long-disconnected nodes
alertSpam.a: In the alert rule settings, use the 'Ignore nodes offline longer than (min)' field. For example, set 1440 (24 hours) — nodes disconnected for more than a day won't trigger repeat alerts.

dockerRestart.q: After docker compose up, services can't see each other
dockerRestart.a:
Make sure the network exists:
docker network create remnawave-network

Then:
docker compose down && docker compose up -d

If using multiple docker-compose files — all must use the same external network.

dbPool.a:
PostgreSQL has a connection limit (default 100). Each service (bot, backend) uses a pool.

Solutions:
1. Reduce DB Pool max size in Settings → Performance
2. Increase max_connections in PostgreSQL:
   ALTER SYSTEM SET max_connections = 200;
3. Restart PostgreSQL

passwordReset.a:
Method 1 — via email (if SMTP configured):
On the login page, click 'Forgot password?' and enter your email.

Method 2 — via CLI:
docker exec -it <container> python3 scripts/admin_cli.py reset-password --username admin

Method 3 — create new superadmin:
docker exec -it <container> python3 scripts/admin_cli.py create-superadmin --username newadmin

smtp.q: How to set up email sending?
smtp.a:
Two options:

1. Built-in mail server — go to Mail Server section, add a domain, configure DNS records (MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC).

2. External SMTP relay — go to Notifications → SMTP Config and enter your SMTP server details (Gmail, Mailgun, etc.).

violationQueue.q: How to tell if the violation detection pipeline is keeping up?
violationQueue.a:
Open /api/v2/collector/stats — it shows:
- pending_users: users waiting for check
- health: idle/ok/busy/overloaded
- backlog: difference between enqueued and processed
- last_drain_duration_ms: last processing time

If health = 'overloaded' (>2000 in queue):
1. Increase 'Chunk size' in Settings → Performance → Pipeline
2. Decrease 'Drain interval' (e.g. from 3 to 1 sec)
3. Increase check cooldown (Violation Detection → Cooldown)

If resources are underutilized but queue grows — increase chunk_size. If CPU is maxed — increase interval and cooldown.

resourceLimits.a:
Resource limits are configured at the Docker level, not the application level.

In docker-compose.yml add:
services:
  web-backend:
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: '2.0'
          memory: 1G
        reservations:
          cpus: '0.5'
          memory: 256M

At the application level, configure: DB pool, sync intervals, cache size — see Performance settings.

violations_hwid_max_accounts.label: 👥 Max accounts per HWID
violations_hwid_max_accounts.description: Number of accounts sharing one HWID that triggers a violation (default 2)

violations_max_unique_ips_per_day.label: 📊 Max unique IPs per day
violations_max_unique_ips_per_day.description: Exceeding this many unique IPs in a day raises a warning (0 = auto from profile)

violations_notification_cooldown_minutes.label: 🔕 Notification cooldown (min)
violations_notification_cooldown_minutes.description: Minimum interval between notifications for the same user

violations_hard_block_ips.label: 🚫 Hard block: max IPs
violations_hard_block_ips.description: Number of unique IPs to trigger an automatic hard block

violations_hard_block_simultaneous.label: 🚫 Hard block: max simultaneous
violations_hard_block_simultaneous.description: Number of simultaneous connections to trigger a hard block

violations_hard_block_devices.label: 🚫 Hard block: max devices
violations_hard_block_devices.description: Number of unique device fingerprints to trigger a hard block

violations_hard_block_hwid_matches.label: 🚫 Hard block: max HWID matches
violations_hard_block_hwid_matches.description: Number of matching HWIDs (same model) to trigger a hard block

violations_trial_tags.label: 🏷️ Trial user tags
violations_trial_tags.description: Comma-separated tags that mark a user as trial (e.g. trial,test,free)

violations_trial_squad_uuids.label: 👥 Trial internal squads
violations_trial_squad_uuids.description: Select internal squads whose users are considered trial

torrent_detection_enabled.label: 🧲 Torrent detection
torrent_detection_enabled.description: Enable/disable torrent traffic detection via Xray routing

torrent_auto_action.label: ⚡ Auto-action on torrent
torrent_auto_action.description: Action on torrent traffic: notify (alert only), block_user (block)

torrent_notification_cooldown_minutes.label: 🔕 Torrent notification cooldown (min)
torrent_notification_cooldown_minutes.description: Minimum interval between torrent notifications for the same user

traffic_rate_enabled.label: 📈 Traffic rate monitor
traffic_rate_enabled.description: Tracks abnormally high traffic consumption over a period

traffic_rate_threshold_gb.label: 📊 Threshold (GB per window)
traffic_rate_threshold_gb.description: Notify when a user consumes more than this amount in the window

traffic_rate_window_minutes.label: ⏱️ Check window (min)
traffic_rate_window_minutes.description: Time window for traffic accounting (default 60 = 1 hour)

traffic_rate_check_interval_minutes.label: 🔁 Check interval (min)
traffic_rate_check_interval_minutes.description: How often to check traffic consumption

traffic_rate_cooldown_minutes.label: 🔕 Notification cooldown (min)
traffic_rate_cooldown_minutes.description: Minimum interval between repeated notifications for the same user

traffic_rate_auto_action.label: ⚡ Auto-action on breach
traffic_rate_auto_action.description: Action on excessive traffic: notify only or auto-block

traffic_rate_auto_block_gb.label: 🚫 Auto-block threshold (GB)
traffic_rate_auto_block_gb.description: Auto-block when traffic in the window exceeds this value. Only takes effect when auto-action = block_user

mailserver_hostname.label: 🖥️ SMTP hostname
mailserver_hostname.description: IP address for the inbound SMTP server (0.0.0.0 = all interfaces)

mailserver_inbound_port.label: 📥 Inbound SMTP port
mailserver_inbound_port.description: Port for inbound mail (default 2525, 25 in production)

mailserver_max_send_per_hour.label: 🚦 Send limit per hour
mailserver_max_send_per_hour.description: Max messages sent per domain per hour

mailserver_queue_poll_interval.label: ⏱️ Queue poll interval
mailserver_queue_poll_interval.description: How often to poll the outbound queue (seconds)

mailserver_max_retries.label: 🔁 Max delivery retries
mailserver_max_retries.description: Maximum number of delivery attempts per message

mailserver_submission_enabled.label: 📤 SMTP Submission enabled
mailserver_submission_enabled.description: Enable SMTP Submission (port 587) for sending mail via login/password

mailserver_submission_port.label: 🔌 SMTP Submission port
mailserver_submission_port.description: Port for the SMTP Submission server (standard — 587)

db_pool_min_size.label: 🗄️ DB Pool: min connections
db_pool_min_size.description: Minimum number of connections in the PostgreSQL pool. Requires restart.

db_pool_max_size.label: 🗄️ DB Pool: max connections
db_pool_max_size.description: Maximum number of connections in the PostgreSQL pool. Increase under heavy load. Requires restart.

db_statement_timeout.label: ⏱️ DB: statement timeout (sec)
db_statement_timeout.description: Maximum SQL statement execution time in seconds. 0 = unlimited. Requires restart.

db_idle_connection_lifetime.label: 💤 DB: idle connection lifetime (sec)
db_idle_connection_lifetime.description: How long idle connections stay in the pool before being closed. Requires restart.

alert_check_interval.label: 🔔 Alert check interval (sec)
alert_check_interval.description: How often to evaluate alert rules. Lower = faster reaction, higher DB load.

config_auto_reload_interval.label: 🔄 Config auto-reload (sec)
config_auto_reload_interval.description: How often to reload configuration from the DB. Affects how quickly setting changes take effect.

api_rate_limit_per_minute.label: 🚦 API: requests/min (global)
api_rate_limit_per_minute.description: Maximum API requests per minute per IP. 0 = unlimited.

collector_rate_limit.label: 📡 Collector: rate limit
collector_rate_limit.description: Rate limit for the node-agent metrics endpoint (format: '1/second').

cache_max_entries.label: 💾 Cache: max entries
cache_max_entries.description: Maximum entries in the in-memory cache. LRU eviction kicks in when exceeded.

cache_default_ttl.label: ⏱️ Cache: default TTL (sec)
cache_default_ttl.description: Default entry TTL. Lower = fresher data, more DB queries.

violation_drain_interval.label: ⏱️ Violation queue: drain interval (sec)
violation_drain_interval.description: How often the worker pulls a chunk of users from the queue. Lower = faster reaction, higher load.

violation_chunk_size.label: 📦 Violation queue: chunk size
violation_chunk_size.description: Users processed per cycle. Higher = queue drains faster, but peak load rises.

violation_max_background_tasks.label: 🔀 Max background tasks
violation_max_background_tasks.description: Maximum concurrent background tasks (torrents, etc.). New tasks are dropped when exceeded.

violation_notification_cooldown_minutes.label: 🔕 Notification cooldown (min)
violation_notification_cooldown_minutes.description: Minimum interval between repeated notifications for the same user. Spam protection.

user_blacklist_enabled.label: 🚫 User blacklist
user_blacklist_enabled.description: Enable Telegram ID blacklist lookup. Matching users are auto-blocked.

user_blacklist_urls.label: 🔗 Blacklist URLs (one per line)
user_blacklist_urls.description: External blacklist file URLs. Format: Telegram ID at the start of each line.

user_blacklist_sync_hours.label: 🔄 Sync interval (hours)
user_blacklist_sync_hours.description: How often to refresh blacklists from external URLs.

user_blacklist_auto_block.label: ⚡ Auto-block
user_blacklist_auto_block.description: Automatically block blacklisted users via Panel API. When disabled — notification only.

legend.title: Value priority
legend.hint: Settings are applied instantly after changes.
legend.applyNote: Settings are applied instantly after changes. To reset, hover over a setting and click X.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.subtitle: Notification center, alert rules and delivery channels

alerts.deleteConfirmDesc: This alert rule will be permanently deleted.
alerts.name: Name
alerts.metricDescCpu: Maximum CPU usage across all nodes
alerts.metricDescRam: Maximum RAM usage across all nodes
alerts.metricDescDisk: Maximum disk usage across all nodes
alerts.metricDescNodeOffline: Minutes since the longest-offline node stopped responding
alerts.metricDescTraffic: Total traffic across all nodes today in GB
alerts.metricDescUsersOnline: Total number of connected users across all nodes
alerts.maxOfflineMinutesHint: Skip alerts for nodes offline longer than this. Useful to stop spam about long-disconnected nodes.
alerts.channelsHint: Select which channels to notify when this rule triggers. Channels must be configured in the Channels tab.
alerts.varIp: IP addresses of offline nodes (alias for node_ips)
alerts.durationMinutesHint: How long the alert stays active before auto-resolving

channels.title: Delivery Channels
channels.noChannelsHint: Add Telegram, Webhook or Email channel to receive notifications outside the panel
channels.webhookHint: Supports Discord, Slack and generic JSON webhooks
channels.emailHint: Requires SMTP to be configured by admin

mailServer.title: Mail Server
mailServer.subtitle: Embedded mail server — send and receive emails from your domain
mailServer.noDomains: No domains configured. Add a domain to start sending emails.
mailServer.deleteDomainConfirm: This will permanently delete this domain and its DKIM keys. Continue?
mailServer.ptrHint: PTR record must be configured at your VPS/hosting provider, not in the domain DNS panel
mailServer.deleteMessageConfirm: This will permanently delete this message. Continue?
mailServer.subject: Subject
mailServer.senderNameHint: Displayed in the 'From' field instead of email address

credentials.title: SMTP Credentials
credentials.subtitle: Manage logins for sending emails via SMTP Submission (port 587)
credentials.noCredentials: No SMTP credentials configured. Create one to allow external services to send emails.
credentials.allowedDomainsHint: Comma-separated. If empty, any configured domain is allowed.
credentials.deleteConfirm: This credential will be permanently deleted. External services will lose access. Continue?

resources.title: Resources
resources.subtitle: Manage API tokens, templates, snippets and config profiles

tokens.description: API tokens for accessing the Remnawave Panel API
tokens.createDescription: Enter a name for the new API token
tokens.tokenCreatedDescription: Copy the token now — it won't be shown again
tokens.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this token? This action cannot be undone.

templates.createDescription: Enter the name and type for the new template
templates.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this template? This action cannot be undone.

snippets.createDescription: Enter the name and JSON content for the new snippet
snippets.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this snippet? This action cannot be undone.

profiles.description: VPN config profiles with inbound configurations
profiles.viewDescription: Full expanded configuration for this profile

billing.title: Billing
billing.subtitle: Manage infrastructure providers, billing history and node payments

providers.createDescription: Enter details for the new infrastructure provider
providers.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this provider? All associated billing records will be affected.

history.description: Track billing payments to infrastructure providers
history.createDescription: Record a payment to an infrastructure provider
history.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this billing record? This action cannot be undone.

nodes.createDescription: Associate a node with a billing provider
nodes.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to remove this billing node association?

reports.title: Reports
reports.subtitle: Violation reports and ASN database management

schedule.globalDescription: Enable/disable all scheduled reports (daily, weekly, monthly)

reports.generateDescription: Generate a new violation report for the selected period

asn.description: Russian ASN database synced from RIPE
asn.searchHint: Search by organization name or filter by provider type

errors.RESET_TOKEN_INVALID: Invalid or expired password reset link
errors.TRAFFIC_LIMIT_REQUIRED: Traffic limit is required. Unlimited traffic is disabled for your role.
errors.TRAFFIC_LIMIT_EXCEEDED: Traffic limit exceeds your quota. Available: {{remaining}} GB
errors.USERS_QUOTA_EXCEEDED: You have reached the maximum number of users allowed for your account.
errors.NODES_QUOTA_EXCEEDED: You have reached the maximum number of nodes allowed for your account.
errors.HOSTS_QUOTA_EXCEEDED: You have reached the maximum number of hosts allowed for your account.
errors.USERNAME_INVALID_FORMAT: Username contains invalid characters. Use 1-100 letters, digits, underscores, hyphens, or dots.
errors.USERNAME_ALREADY_EXISTS: A user with this username already exists.
errors.TRAFFIC_LIMIT_TOO_SMALL: Traffic limit is too small. Minimum is 1 MB.
errors.INVALID_TELEGRAM_ID: Telegram ID is not valid (must be 1-9999999999).
errors.INVALID_HWID_DEVICE_LIMIT: HWID device limit must be 0 (unlimited) or a positive integer.
errors.INVALID_EXPIRE_DATE: Expiration date must be in the future.
errors.PANEL_REJECTED_TRAFFIC_STRATEGY: Panel rejected the traffic limit strategy.
errors.PANEL_USER_ALREADY_EXISTS: A user with this username already exists in the panel.
errors.TRAFFIC_LIMIT_BELOW_USAGE: Cannot set traffic limit below current usage.

backup.title: Backups
backup.subtitle: Database backups, config export/import, and user import
backup.confirmDeleteDesc: Are you sure you want to delete "{{filename}}"? This action cannot be undone.
backup.confirmRestoreDesc: This will overwrite the current database with the backup "{{filename}}". This action cannot be undone. Are you sure?
backup.noConfigFiles: No config backup files available. Create a config backup first.
backup.noUserFiles: No user export files available. Upload a JSON file to the backup directory.
backup.importUsersWarning: User import will create new users via the Remnawave Panel API. Existing users with the same username will be skipped.
backup.servicesTopicHint: Sent to the "Services" topic from notification settings.
backup.configDesc: Full panel configuration including users, nodes, hosts, settings
backup.noFilesDesc: No backup files found. Create one to get started.

apiKeys.title: API Keys & Webhooks
apiKeys.subtitle: Manage external API access and webhook subscriptions
apiKeys.keyCreatedWarning: Copy this key now. You won't be able to see it again.
apiKeys.confirmDeleteDesc: This will permanently revoke this API key. Any integrations using it will stop working.
apiKeys.confirmDeleteWebhookDesc: This will permanently delete this webhook subscription.
apiKeys.confirmSaved: I have saved this key in a secure place
apiKeys.urlInvalid: URL must start with http:// or https://
apiKeys.secretHint: Signature is sent in X-Webhook-Signature header (HMAC-SHA256)
apiKeys.descriptionPlaceholder: What is this key used for?
apiKeys.confirmRotateDesc: The current key will be invalidated immediately. Any clients using it will need to be updated.
apiKeys.autoDisabled: Auto-disabled after too many failed attempts
apiKeys.signatureHint: HMAC-SHA256 over the request body, included in the X-Signature header

forcePassword.title: Password change required
forcePassword.description: Your password was set by an administrator. For security, please set your own password.

squads.title: Squads
squads.subtitle: Manage internal and external squads for user grouping
squads.name: Squad name
squads.deleteDescription: This will remove the squad. Users will be unassigned.

bedolaga.title: Bedolaga Bot
bedolaga.subtitle: Bedolaga Bot statistics and monitoring
bedolaga.tooltip: Data is fetched from Bedolaga Bot API in real time
bedolaga.notConfiguredDesc: Add BEDOLAGA_API_URL and BEDOLAGA_API_TOKEN environment variables to connect to the Bedolaga Bot API.

customers.title: Bedolaga Customers
customers.subtitle: Manage bot customers
customers.searchPlaceholder: Search by name or Telegram ID...

customerDetail.name: Name
customerDetail.extendDesc: Enter number of days to extend

referrals.title: Referral Network
referrals.subtitle: Referrers and the customers they have brought in
referrals.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, username or referral code...
referrals.noRefsForUser: This referrer hasn't brought anyone yet

promo.title: Promo Codes
promo.subtitle: Manage Bedolaga Bot promo codes
promo.deleteConfirmText: Are you sure you want to delete promo code {{code}}? This action cannot be undone.

marketing.title: Marketing
marketing.subtitle: Bedolaga Bot campaigns and mailings
marketing.deleteCampaignText: Are you sure you want to delete campaign "{{name}}"?
marketing.sendCampaignText: Start sending campaign "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.

notFound.title: Page Not Found
notFound.description: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

resetPassword.title: Reset Password
resetPassword.description: Enter the email associated with your account. We'll send a password reset link.
resetPassword.emailSentDescription: If this email is associated with an account, you'll receive a password reset link. Check your inbox, including spam folder.
resetPassword.newPasswordDescription: Create a new password for your account
resetPassword.successDescription: Your password has been reset successfully. You can now log in with your new password.
resetPassword.error: An error occurred. Please try again later.

accessPolicies.selectHint: Pick a template on the left or create a new one
accessPolicies.name: Name
accessPolicies.noRules: No rules — this template is empty and doesn't restrict anything. Add a rule to define a whitelist.
accessPolicies.roleHint: All admins with these roles inherit the template's rules.
accessPolicies.adminHint: Template applies only to the selected admins (on top of role attachments).
accessPolicies.confirmDelete: Delete this template? Admins who inherited it will lose these rights.
accessPolicies.errNoScopeValue: Fill in scope value for every rule
accessPolicies.errNoAction: Select at least one action in every rule

deleteConfirm.description: This access policy will be removed. Any admins and roles using it will lose their restrictions.
deleteConfirm.title: Delete access policy?

adminPlugins.title: Plugins
adminPlugins.subtitle: Manage paid add-ons: upload wheel, set license, uninstall.
adminPlugins.restart_hint: Restart the backend after any change to apply it.
adminPlugins.empty: No plugins installed. Upload a wheel from the vendor to start.
adminPlugins.pending_wheel: Wheel found but no license attached — provide a JWT via Install or Master key.

install_dialog.title: Install plugin
install_dialog.subtitle: Upload the wheel file and paste the vendor-issued JWT. The plugin activates after a restart.
install_dialog.plugin_id_hint: Same id the vendor uses (e.g. smart_support).

master_dialog.title: Master key
master_dialog.subtitle: A single JWT can cover several plugins. Paste it and the panel will fan it out to every plugin it licenses.

license_dialog.title: Update license: {{name}}
license_dialog.subtitle: Paste a new JWT for this plugin.

uninstall_dialog.title: Remove plugin
uninstall_dialog.subtitle: Plugin "{{name}}" will be removed together with its license. This is irreversible. Continue?

restart_dialog.title: Restart backend?
restart_dialog.subtitle: The backend will shut down and the container should bring it back automatically (if docker-compose has restart: unless-stopped). Panel will be briefly unavailable.

license.missing_body: Install your Smart Support key in settings to continue.
license.expired_body: Renew your subscription to use the debugger again.

search.title: Smart Support — user lookup
search.subtitle: UUID, short_uuid, email, IP, Telegram ID or a chunk of the username — we'll find them.
search.placeholder: Paste any identifier from the ticket…
search.hint: Any free-form string works — the resolver picks the right lookup automatically.
search.empty: Nothing matched. Try a shorter or different query.

report.client_unknown: Client app could not be inferred from recent subscription requests.
report.nodes_empty: No nodes touched in the last 24 hours.
report.correlations_empty: No correlations yet — the background worker isn't running or no patterns matched.
report.violations_empty: No violations recorded in the last 14 days.
report.sessions_empty: No actions on this user yet.

fields.hwid_blacklisted: Warning: one device is on the blacklist.
fields.outdated_warning: Client version is outdated — ask the user to update.

audit.title: Audit log
audit.subtitle: Every quick action triggered through Smart Support. Append-only — entries can't be edited or removed.

params.revoke_only_passwords: Rotate proxy passwords only (keep the subscription URL)

node_overloaded.title: Node overloaded
node_overloaded.detail: CPU/RAM are near the limit — the user may experience slowness.

node_disconnected.title: Node unavailable
node_disconnected.detail: The active node is offline or hasn't reported metrics in a while.

subscription_expired.title: Subscription expired
subscription_expired.detail: The plan has lapsed — the user is disconnected by design.

traffic_exhausted.title: Traffic exhausted
traffic_exhausted.detail: The user has used up the traffic quota of their plan.

client_version_outdated.title: Client outdated
client_version_outdated.detail: An older version of the app is installed — bugs may be involved.

affected_by_cluster.title: Mass incident
affected_by_cluster.detail: User is part of an active incident cluster — several users affected at once.

settings.title: Smart Support settings
settings.subtitle: Rule firing thresholds and correlation worker schedule. Change with care.
settings.save_error: Could not save — please try again

ai.chain_hint: Comma-separated list. Allowed: gemini, groq, openrouter. When the first one runs out of free-tier limits, the request falls through to the next.
ai.key_placeholder_set: Enter a new key to replace it

node_cpu_high.label: CPU threshold, %
node_cpu_high.help: "Node overloaded" fires when CPU >= this value.

node_cpu_critical.label: Critical CPU, %
node_cpu_critical.help: Hypothesis confidence climbs once this threshold is exceeded.

node_memory_high.label: RAM threshold, %
node_memory_high.help: "Node overloaded" fires when memory usage >= this value.

node_metrics_stale_seconds.label: Metrics stale after, sec
node_metrics_stale_seconds.help: Treat the node as stuck if its last update is older than this.

traffic_high.label: Traffic high threshold, %
traffic_high.help: "Traffic exhausted" fires once usage >= this percent.

traffic_high_confidence.label: Confidence (high)
traffic_high_confidence.help: Hypothesis confidence between high and full (0..1).

traffic_full_confidence.label: Confidence (full)
traffic_full_confidence.help: Hypothesis confidence at the full mark (0..1).

cluster_node_window_minutes.label: Node window, min
cluster_node_window_minutes.help: How recent reconnect spikes on a single node we look at.

cluster_node_reconnects_per_user.label: Reconnects per user
cluster_node_reconnects_per_user.help: Reconnect count inside the window to count a user toward a cluster.

cluster_node_min_affected.label: Min users in cluster (node)
cluster_node_min_affected.help: Unique users needed for the node cluster to become hot.

cluster_asn_window_minutes.label: ASN window, min
cluster_asn_window_minutes.help: How far back we collect violations for ASN clusters.

cluster_asn_min_affected.label: Min users in cluster (ASN)
cluster_asn_min_affected.help: Unique users needed for the ASN cluster to become hot.

correlation_recompute_seconds.label: Recompute interval, sec
correlation_recompute_seconds.help: Takes effect on the next panel restart.

correlation_max_age_minutes.label: Cache TTL, min
correlation_max_age_minutes.help: Older clusters are not surfaced in reports even if still in DB.

xray.profileSaveFailed: Failed to send config to panel
xray.pushBlockedDesc: Balancer "{{tag}}" has no target outbounds. Node will crash if you push this.
xray.fixValidationBeforeSaving: Please fix validation errors before saving
xray.fillUrlAndToken: Please fill URL and API Token
xray.jsonNoValidOutbounds: JSON array detected, but no valid outbounds found
xray.failedDownloadDatabase: Failed to download database for extraction
xray.hwidDeviceLimitDesc: Device limit reached. Check 'Active Devices' in the panel.
xray.jsonNoValidOutboundsInSub: JSON detected but no valid outbounds found inside
xray.noValidLinksInResponse: No valid links found in response
xray.regenerateHwidConfirm: Regenerate HWID? The panel will see this as a NEW device.
xray.noValidLinksToImport: No valid links or JSON configs found to import
xray.warpAccountFailedDesc: CORS error or proxy is down. Check your settings.
